changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Going Smoothly"

TextENGNew: The sound of the car grew closer, followed by the sound of it braking as the engine cut out.
At that instant, the man in the room -- who had been sprawled out lazily -- shot upright, pressed himself against the wall beside the window, and cautiously peered outside...
changeReason: Old misinterprets 足を投げ出していた (had legs sprawled out lazily) as "lackadaisical footsteps"; the man was sitting/lying with legs stretched out, not walking.

TextENGNew: ...It was his comrade's car.
Even so, he still didn't relax his guard.
Footsteps approached the door.@k...Thump, thump-thump-thump. The prearranged knock.
changeReason: "Passcode" changed to "prearranged knock" -- 決められた合図のノック means a prearranged signal knock, not a verbal passcode.

TextENGNew: "...I'm back. Open up, it's me."
changeReason: Old omits 俺だ ("it's me") and adds "already" implying impatience not strongly present in the Japanese; the dialect rendering also differs.

TextENGNew: ``Ahh, you must be tired. I'm opening it now.``
The locks were undone and the door opened, revealing a man with his arms full of bulging supermarket plastic bags.
The bags bore the logo "Seventh Mart," with bread rolls and milk cartons peeking out from the top.
The contents of those bags were then spread out over a sheet on the floor.
"I bought some cup noodles, so boil some water. ...How's the kid?"
``Hm? He's been sleeping the entire time.@k@vS20/00/yamaB44003. Thank god he's not causing any trouble.@k@vS20/00/yamaB44004. He did struggle a bit when he was about to shit himself.``
"Don' let 'im soil himself. The stench would be real bad if someone came snoopin' around."
changeReason: Old says the stench would be bad "in here" but the Japanese 万一の時 (in case of emergency) implies concern about detection by outsiders; new correctly captures this as "if someone came snoopin' around."

TextENGNew: ``...Yeah, I know.``
"Check the gag regularly. Can't have it comin' loose, but don' make it too tight neither...@k@vS20/14/443400014.If he suffocates, this was all for nothin'."
``I said, I know... Huh? Didn't I ask you to pick up another can of gas for the portable stove?@k@vS20/00/yamaB44007. We're out.``
``Ya never asked, ya ijit.``
``...Gaah... seriously? Come on, work.... Dammit!!``
He rattled the portable stove as he struggled to get it to light.
...Seeing that, the man who went to buy supplies let out a deep sigh.
And with a backwards glance at that sight, began to walk to the corner of the room.
...The kidnapped boy lay sprawled on a blanket spread over the floor.
changeReason: "Sheet" changed to "blanket" -- 毛布 specifically means blanket, not sheet.

TextENGNew: ``....Boy, ya doin' okay?``
Of course, the man didn't assume the kid was able to hear that question.
Because the boy's ears were plugged, and his eyes and ears were thoroughly sealed beneath layers of packing tape.
Also, his mouth was gagged with a thin, twisted-up towel.
...Because of that, the boy was unable to close his mouth properly, his cheeks sticky with his own saliva.
Of course, that wasn't the only reason.
Both his arms were tied tightly behind his back with a leather belt.
"Things seem to be goin' smooth. Yer life ain't in danger.@k@vS20/14/443400018....If yer grandpa had tried to play tough, we mighta had to lop off an ear... be grateful we don' have to..."

"If the Main Family decides somethin'... there's no arguin'. ...I can't even imagine what kinda cruel orders they might give.@k@vS20/14/443400020....But they said not a single scratch on 'im, so things must be goin' well for now..."

``The Minister is discretely withdrawing the dam project. The Hinamizawa Dam is indefinitely postponed.@k@vS20/00/yamaB44010. ...I wonder when we're letting the boy go.@k@vS20/00/yamaB44011. ...I just want to be done with this already.``
``Tha Main Family seem ta be gaugin' tha timin'. I don' know when it'll be, but prob'ly soon.``
``Isn't that great, kid? You'll be let go real soon, heh heh heh...``
The men didn't know if their voices were heard by the boy.
...For the boy, there was nothing he could do but keep sleeping -- the only defense he had left to shield his spirit from this wretched reality...
changeReason: Old omits 魂を守るために (to protect his spirit) and 自衛の方法 (means of self-defense), which the new translation includes, adding emotional depth about the boy's desperate psychological retreat.

TextENGNew: ``More importan'ly, wha' are we gonna do about tha gas? We can' eat the cup noodles raw!!@k@vS20/14/443400023. If we're out of gas say something, geez!!``
